description: Machine-readable cross-link catalog for the m-dev-tools GitHub organization. Authoritative map from user intent (task_index) to repo + tool + command (tools). Designed to be the first file an AI agent reads after llms.txt when asked to build an M (MUMPS) application using the m-dev-tools stack.

org.name: m-dev-tools
org.tagline: Engine-neutral M (MUMPS) developer tools — TDD + CI/CD stack for InterSystems IRIS and YottaDB.
first_principles: m-stdlib has architectural priority over m-cli — when both projects need a utility, it lands in m-stdlib first.
Source-level tools (m fmt, m lint) are engine-neutral; runtime tools (m test, m coverage) are YottaDB-targeted.
Every module ships with a vendored conformance corpus tied to the relevant RFC or NIST publication.
m-cli's CLI ergonomics deliberately mirror Go (`go fmt`/`test`/`vet`) and Rust (`cargo fmt`/`test`/`clippy`) — see m-tools/docs/m-tool-gap-analysis.md for the gap analysis that drove the design.

m-cli.role: Canonical CLI toolchain — m fmt / lint / test / coverage / watch / lsp / doc / new / ...

watch.purpose: Re-run affected tests on file change (sub-second TDD loop)

coverage.purpose: Line + label coverage via YDB view "TRACE"

lsp.purpose: Language Server over stdio — diagnostics, formatting, code actions, hover, completion, document symbols, code lenses, folding, signature help, document highlight, go-to-definition, find-references, workspace symbols

doc.purpose: Lookup STD* symbol docs by name

search.purpose: Full-text search across the m-stdlib manifest

manifest.purpose: Print the active m-stdlib manifest (or filter by module)

examples.purpose: Print runnable examples for a STD* module

errors.purpose: Print error catalogue for a STD* module

new.purpose: Scaffold a TDD-ready M project (Makefile, .m-cli.toml, tests/, CI)

plugins.purpose: List m_cli.plugins entry-points (e.g. m-cli-extras' m corpus-stats)

m-cli-extras.role: Out-of-tree m-cli plugins — niche subcommands registered via the m_cli.plugins entry-point group

corpus-stats.purpose: Walk a directory of .m files; report file / line / label / parse-error counts
use_cases: Sizing rule profiles before lint runs

m-stdlib.role: Pure-M (and selectively $ZF-bound) runtime standard library — STD* modules

machine_readable.manifest_role: Per-label structured docs (signature, params, returns, raises, since, stable, see-also, examples, source.line). Consumed by m-cli's `m doc/search/examples/errors` and by m-stdlib-vscode for hover/goto-def/completion.

m-stdlib.doc_grammar: M-doc tag grammar v1: @param / @returns / @raises / @example / @since / @stable / @see / @deprecated / @internal — see docs/guides/m-doc-grammar.md

STDASSERT.headline: Assertion library — eq / ne / true / false / near / raises / contains / len + counter start / report. Wire-protocol-compatible with the m-cli test runner.

STDFIX.headline: Fixture lifecycle — with / invoke / register / cleanup. Powers `m test`'s default per-test isolation.

STDSEED.headline: TSV / JSON manifest loader for FileMan record fixtures + pluggable filer hook.

STDSNAP.headline: Snapshot testing — serialize / save / matches / asserts; canonical line-per-leaf dump via $QUERY.

STDPROF.headline: Wall-clock profiler — start / stop / count / total / mean / min / max / percentile / tags.

STDJSON.headline: RFC 8259 JSON parser + serializer — one M tree node per JSON value.

STDREGEX.headline: Thompson-NFA regex engine — literals, classes, groups, alternation, greedy quantifiers, capture, replace, split.

STDXML.headline: XML 1.0 parser — elements, attributes, CDATA, comments / PI / xml-decl, namespaces, full XPath subset.

STDCSV.headline: RFC 4180 CSV parser/writer — every §2 clause, optional file I/O.

STDTOML.headline: TOML 1.0 subset — top-level pairs + [section] tables; string / int / float / bool scalars.

STDB64.headline: RFC 4648 Base64 — standard alphabet + URL-safe variant + valid predicate.

STDHEX.headline: RFC 4648 §8 hex — lowercase default, uppercase variant, case-insensitive decode.

STDUUID.headline: RFC 4122 v4 + RFC 9562 v7 UUIDs (v7 sorts in generation order).

STDCRYPTO.headline: SHA-256/384/512 + HMAC-SHA-256/384/512 backed by OpenSSL EVP_Digest + HMAC.

STDCSPRNG.headline: Cryptographic random — bytes / hex / base64 / token / int / uuid4 backed by /dev/urandom.

STDCOMPRESS.headline: gzip / gunzip / deflate / inflate / zstdCompress / zstdDecompress + magic-byte autodetect.

STDHTTP.headline: HTTP/1.1 client — $$get / $$post / $$request driving libcurl, plus pure-M wire-format helpers.

STDURL.headline: RFC 3986 URI parse / build / encode / decode / valid / normalize / resolve.

STDDATE.headline: ISO-8601 datetime + duration arithmetic — now / fromh / toh / strftime / strptime / add / diff.

STDLOG.headline: Structured logger — five levels, four sinks, kv or json line format.

STDFS.headline: File-system primitives — text + byte-faithful read/write/append, exists/remove/size, basename/dirname/join.

STDOS.headline: Process / env / cmdline helpers — env / pid / cmdline / argv / cwd / user / hostname / exit.

STDENV.headline: .env loader + typed accessors — parse / parseFile / valid / has / get / getInt / getBool / getFloat.

STDARGS.headline: argparse — long/short/grouped flags, positionals, sub-commands, `--` terminator.

STDFMT.headline: Printf-style formatter — subset of Python str.format (fill / align / width / precision / type s d f x X o b).

STDSTR.headline: String helpers — pad / trim / replaceAll / split / startsWith / endsWith / case / repeat.

STDCOLL.headline: Collections — Set / Map / Stack / Queue / Deque / Heap / OrderedDict over caller-owned arrays.

STDCACHE.headline: LRU + TTL cache over caller-owned array — new / put / get / has / remove / clear.

STDXFRM.headline: Higher-order array transforms — map / filter / reduce via XECUTE-evaluated lambdas.

STDMATH.headline: Numeric helpers — clamp / min / max / sum / count / mean over caller-owned arrays.

STDSEMVER.headline: SemVer 2.0.0 — valid / parse / compare / matches with caret, tilde, comparator AND-combination.

m-standard.role: Citable, machine-readable M language reference reconciling four primary sources

machine_readable.schema_version_pin: Downstream consumers pin schema_version="1" (per ADR-005). Additive changes flow through; breaking changes bump.

three_layered_standards.pragmatic: What runs unmodified on both YDB and IRIS (81 tokens)
three_layered_standards.va_sac: What the VA permits in VistA code (65 rules / 171 per-name flags)
three_layered_standards.operational: Pragmatic ∩ SAC — what a VistA developer can use (58 tokens)

tree-sitter-m.role: Tree-sitter grammar for M — generated mechanically from m-standard's grammar-surface.json

m-test-engine.role: Minimal YottaDB Docker container — the lightweight default test substrate for non-VistA M development

lifecycle.make up: Build image (if needed) and start container detached
lifecycle.make down: Stop and remove container; preserves globals volume
lifecycle.make clean: Destructive — stop + remove + delete globals volume

m-test-engine.alternative: vista-meta (heavyweight, VistA + FileMan + RPC + Octo SQL) — keep for VistA-specific work

tree-sitter-m-vscode.role: VS Code extension — syntax highlighting via tree-sitter-m WASM + LSP client (spawns `m lsp` from m-cli)
commands: Open all M files in workspace as tabs
Smoke-report all M files in workspace

m-stdlib-vscode.role: VS Code extension — manifest-driven hover / goto-def / completion for STD* symbols in .m files

m-modern-corpus.role: Validation corpus — modern non-VistA M source from active open-source projects
m-modern-corpus.calibrates: M-MOD-NN lint rule track (target ~3 findings/routine on default profile vs ~57 for legacy XINDEX rules)

m-tools.role: ARCHIVED — historical seed of the entire org. Original gap analysis + Tier 1–4 strategy + canonical command map.
m-tools.read_for: Why the ecosystem is shaped the way it is. See m-tools/docs/m-tool-gap-analysis.md for the Go/Rust/Python comparison that drove m-cli's CLI ergonomics.

task_index._comment: Plain-English user intent → primary tool/module + see-alsos + minimal example. Use this as the first lookup when a user asks 'how do I X in M?'

scaffold_new_project.intent: Start a new M project with TDD/CI scaffolding

run_all_tests.intent: Run every test suite in the project

run_one_test.intent: Run a single test by file::label

watch_loop.intent: Sub-second TDD feedback — rerun affected tests on save

translate_compact_to_canonical.intent: Expand VistA-compact code (S X=1) to canonical names (SET X=1)

scaffold_ci.intent: Drop a CI workflow into the project

pre_commit.intent: Block bad commits with fmt-check + lint

self_check.intent: Verify ydb / parser / m-standard / manifests resolve

lookup_stdlib_symbol.intent: What does parse^STDJSON do? Show signature + examples

editor_setup.intent: Configure VS Code for M development

snapshot_testing.intent: Compare structured output against a saved snapshot

profiling.intent: Wall-clock measure spans of M code

json_parse.intent: Parse JSON text into an M tree

json_serialize.intent: Serialize an M tree to JSON text

xml_parse.intent: Parse XML; query with XPath subset

http_client.note: client only — m-stdlib does not ship an HTTP server

datetime.intent: Work with ISO-8601 dates and durations

uuid.intent: Generate UUIDs (v4 random or v7 sortable)

collections.intent: Set / Map / Stack / Queue / Heap / OrderedDict

fixtures_fileman.intent: Load FileMan record fixtures from TSV/JSON manifest

is_keyword_portable.intent: Does this command/function run on both YDB and IRIS?

is_keyword_ansi_standard.intent: Is this part of the ANSI M standard?

is_keyword_va_sac_clean.intent: Does the VA SAC permit this in VistA code?

all_keywords_for_grammar.intent: Need every command/function/ISV with provenance for a parser/linter

ydb_specific_token.intent: Which $Z* / Z* tokens are YDB extensions?

iris_specific_token.intent: Which $Z* / Z* tokens are IRIS extensions?

docker_yottadb.intent: Run YottaDB in a container for tests

test_corpus_modern.intent: Validate against modern non-VistA M code

why_does_m-dev-tools_exist.intent: Read the original gap analysis that justified the org

go_rust_python_analogues.intent: How does m-cli map to go/cargo/poetry?

tier1_strategy.intent: What was the original Tier 1 deliverable scope?

tdd_inner_loop.description: Canonical red → green → refactor loop using m-dev-tools. Mirrors `cargo new && cargo test && cargo clippy && cargo fmt` end to end.

steps.action: Create a new M project with Makefile, .m-cli.toml, tests/, CI
Write a failing test in tests/MYRTNTST.m using STDASSERT
Implement the routine until tests pass
Optional — leave watch running for sub-second feedback
steps.note: skip steps 4–6 manually after this; the watcher reruns affected suites on save

steps.action: Push — CI runs the same gates via `make ci`

minimal_application_recipe.description: Smallest realistic project: parse a JSON HTTP response and log it. Hits STDHTTP + STDJSON + STDLOG end-to-end.

steps.action: Write tests/FETCHTST.m with STDASSERT + STDMOCK (mock STDHTTP)
Implement src/fetch.m using STDHTTP for the call, STDJSON for parsing, STDLOG for output

steps.command: m test && m fmt && m lint

discovery_protocol._comment: How an AI agent should consume this catalog when given a fresh user request.
step_1_read: llms.txt — human-sized AI entrypoint and priority reading order
tools.schema.json — validation contract for this catalog
discovery_protocol.step_2_match_intent: Map the user's plain-English request to one or more task_index keys. Prefer exact keys first, then fuzzy keyword match against each `intent` string.
discovery_protocol.step_3_resolve_tools: For each matched task, follow `primary` and `see_also` references into the `tools` map. Treat `repo`, `docs`, `subcommands`, `machine_readable`, and `modules` as routing metadata.
discovery_protocol.step_4_load_local_context: Before changing a repo, read that repo's README plus CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md when present. Repo-local instructions override org-level generalities.
discovery_protocol.step_5_load_manifests: For symbol-level work, fetch the relevant manifest or machine_readable URL. Use m-stdlib's manifest for STD* APIs and m-standard's grammar/tables for language semantics.
discovery_protocol.step_6_compose_workflow: Use `workflow.tdd_inner_loop` for new application work: scaffold, write failing test, implement, run test, fmt, lint, coverage, CI.
discovery_protocol.step_7_verify: Run the nearest available toolchain checks (`m test`, `m fmt --check`, `m lint`, `m coverage`, `m doctor`) before declaring completion.
discovery_protocol.step_8_do_not_invent: If a requested STD* API, m-cli command, lint rule, or language token is absent from the relevant manifest/catalog, do not fabricate it. State the gap and route the addition to the owning repo.
